posts.raw_content:
God wants you to know that He keeps watch over His people (You and Me), even Kings and world leaders

“I saw in the visions of my head upon my bed, and, behold, a watcher and an holy one came down from heaven.” (Daniel 4:13)

"The eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry." (Psalm 34:15)

This angelic decree ends by stating the purpose of the humiliating events that would happen to Nebuchadnezzar, the head of the world’s first great empire.  (Daniel 4:14–16) The purpose of these events is to teach Nebuchadnezzar humility before God. Despite the king's arrogance (Daniel 3:15) and great power (Daniel 2:36–38), the Lord gives ability to whomever He chooses. Compared to the Lord God, any ruler is just a lowly, limited human being. Nebuchadnezzar had considered himself a god with vast authority over a vast empire. His pride prompted him to set up an idol on the plain of Dura and force his subjects to worship it (Daniel 3:1–6). However, the Lord planned to teach Nebuchadnezzar that God is the supreme Ruler of everything in heaven and on earth.

This verse speaks of the "watchers" as a group, and the judgment to be a "decree" and "decision" of that group. However, this does not imply that the collection of spiritual beings decided on this result. Nor does it mean the judgment is enforced on their authority. The first phrase of this verse is composed only of nouns. A more poetic and literalist rendering might be simply to state, "the decree of the watchers, a sentence, a command of the holy ones, a decision." The impression is that these angelic voices are declaring something: they are announcing it officially, indicating that it has been decided.

"The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry") means God is actively watching over, protecting, and attentive to the prayers of those who trust in Him. It highlights God's mercy and personal care for the faithful, contrasting with His opposition to evil. It comforts us to know that He hears and answers the cries, heartaches, and prayers of His people, especially in times of suffering. King David wrote this while fleeing for his life, expressing confidence that God is near the brokenhearted and will deliver them.

"He keeps the paths of judgment, and preserves the way of his saints." (Proverbs 2:8)

posts.content:
God wants you to know that He keeps watch over His people (You and Me), even Kings and world leaders

“I saw in the visions of my head upon my bed, and, behold, a watcher and an holy one came down from heaven.” (Daniel 4:13)

"The eyes of the Lord are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry." (Psalm 34:15)

This angelic decree ends by stating the purpose of the humiliating events that would happen to Nebuchadnezzar, the head of the world’s first great empire. (Daniel 4:14–16) The purpose of these events is to teach Nebuchadnezzar humility before God. Despite the king's arrogance (Daniel 3:15) and great power (Daniel 2:36–38), the Lord gives ability to whomever He chooses. Compared to the Lord God, any ruler is just a lowly, limited human being. Nebuchadnezzar had considered himself a god with vast authority over a vast empire. His pride prompted him to set up an idol on the plain of Dura and force his subjects to worship it (Daniel 3:1–6). However, the Lord planned to teach Nebuchadnezzar that God is the supreme Ruler of everything in heaven and on earth.

This verse speaks of the "watchers" as a group, and the judgment to be a "decree" and "decision" of that group. However, this does not imply that the collection of spiritual beings decided on this result. Nor does it mean the judgment is enforced on their authority. The first phrase of this verse is composed only of nouns. A more poetic and literalist rendering might be simply to state, "the decree of the watchers, a sentence, a command of the holy ones, a decision." The impression is that these angelic voices are declaring something: they are announcing it officially, indicating that it has been decided.

"The eyes of the LORD are upon the righteous, and his ears are open unto their cry") means God is actively watching over, protecting, and attentive to the prayers of those who trust in Him. It highlights God's mercy and personal care for the faithful, contrasting with His opposition to evil. It comforts us to know that He hears and answers the cries, heartaches, and prayers of His people, especially in times of suffering. King David wrote this while fleeing for his life, expressing confidence that God is near the brokenhearted and will deliver them.

"He keeps the paths of judgment, and preserves the way of his saints." (Proverbs 2:8)

posts.title: Tucker Carlson Backpedals On Trump ‘Antichrist’ Comments In Heated NYT Sit-Down

link_metadata.description: Conservative media personality Tucker Carlson denied ever labeling Donald Trump the “antichrist” during a tense exchange with New York Times journalist Lulu Garcia-Navarro, only to backtrack when confronted with his own recent broadcast scripts. The confrontation, featured on the Times’ program The Interview, highlights a growing rift between Carlson and the president, fueled by disagreements over foreign policy in Iran and controversial social media depictions of the two figures.
